subject: Fwd: Update
body:

From: "Steven Storch" <sstorch@samlegal.com>
Subject: Update
Date: Wed, 25 Jun 2008 21:19:31 GMT
To: dkiesq@aol.com

Darren, I spoke with David Anders. He is going to go back again to
Spector to see if he is interested in talking but I doubt anything will
come of it.  His position remains that Spector was not involved and that
Bear Stearns should cover any losses. I also asked Anders if he would
accept  service of the complaint and he will speak with Spector
regarding that as well. I told him I needed to hear from him this week
or we would proceed without further notice.

Condren is out of the country until next week. He sent me an email (
attached) . His assistant says he will be back next week. I propose to
send Condren the following:

 

Jim, thank you for your email and for interrupting your vacation to get
back to me. Given all the developments in the Bear Stearns' matter I am
concerned that my clients will be prejudiced by any delay. Accordingly,
while we are interested in seeing if this matter can be resolved short
of litigation, we will need to  promptly commence action in order to
protect their rights. We are prepared to meet early  next week to see
whether or not we can resolve this matter. Absent  such a meeting, and a
prompt resolution, we will have no alternative but to proceed to
litigation.

 

I look forward to hearing back from you upon your return next week .

 

Steven G. Storch

Storch Amini & Munves, P.C.

2 Grand Central Tower

140 E. 45th Street

New York, New York 10017

Tel: (212) 490-4100 ext 216

Fax: (212) 490-4208

 

Confidentiality Notice: The information contained in this e-mail and any
attachments may be legally privileged and confidential. If you have
received this e-mail in error, please notify the sender and permanently
delete the e-mail and any attachments immediately. You should not
retain, copy or use this e-mail or any attachment for any purpose, nor
disclose all or any part of the contents to any person. Thank you.

From:Steven Storch <sstorch@samlegal.com>
Subject:Update
Date:Wed, 25 Jun 2008 21:19:31 GMT
To:dkiesq@aol.com

Darren, I spoke with David Anders. He is going to go back again to Spector to see if he is interested in talking but I doubt anything will come of it.  His position remains that Spector was not involved and that Bear Stearns should cover any losses. I also asked Anders if he would accept  service of the complaint and he will speak with Spector regarding that as well. I told him I needed to hear from him this week or we would proceed without further notice.

Condren is out of the country until next week. He sent me an email ( attached) . His assistant says he will be back next week. I propose to send Condren the following:

 

Jim, thank you for your email and for interrupting your vacation to get back to me. Given all the developments in the Bear Stearns’ matter I am concerned that my clients will be prejudiced by any delay. Accordingly, while we are interested in seeing if this matter can be resolved short of litigation, we will need to  promptly commence action in order to protect their rights. We are prepared to meet early  next week to see whether or not we can resolve this matter. Absent  such a meeting, and a  prompt resolution, we will have no alternative but to proceed to litigation.

 

I look forward to hearing back from you upon your return next week .

 

Steven G. Storch

Storch Amini & Munves, P.C.

2 Grand Central Tower

140 E. 45th Street

New York, New York 10017

Tel: (212) 490-4100 ext 216

Fax: (212) 490-4208

You should not retain, copy or use this e-mail or any attachment for any purpose, nor disclose all or any part of the contents to any person. Thank you.
